summary: Validated contact email template builders by code inspection and local TypeScript execution only (no live /api/contact/send call). Admin and author templates are differentiated and match requested content/tone/branding.

minor.issue: footer/site URL source uses NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAT_API_URL, which appears chat-API oriented and may not always match the public website link expected in email signature.

action_items: Confirm whether signature/footer URL should come from the main site URL env (not NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAT_API_URL) to avoid wrong links in outbound emails.
critical_code_review_comments: Email template responsibilities are cleanly separated via buildAdminContactRequestBody and buildAuthorConfirmationRequestBody.
HTML escaping is correctly applied before interpolation in both templates.
Potential config coupling risk: NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAT_API_URL naming suggests API endpoint, not canonical website URL used in signature/CTA.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live send endpoint intentionally not invoked to avoid real emails. Validation done via direct execution of exported builders using tsx runner. Verified admin contains coordinates+subject+full message, author contains warm confirmation + message copy, and both share brand shell/signature.
rca of the issue: No functional template bug reproduced. Only configuration risk identified: site URL in email shell is sourced from NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAT_API_URL; if this env points to API/chat host, email CTA/signature links can be incorrect.
